title: Ratio Analysis: A company has a gross profit of 150000 and total...
summary: Calculate gross profit margin and net profit margin from revenue and expense data.
prompt: A company has a gross profit of $150,000 and total sales of $600,000. Calculate the gross profit margin. If the company’s operating expenses total $70,000, calculate the net profit margin.
correct_option: Gross Profit Margin = 25%; Net Profit Margin = 13.33%
answer_text: Gross Profit Margin = 25%; Net Profit Margin = 13.33%
solution: Step 1: Gross Profit Margin = Gross Profit / Total Sales = $150,000 / $600,000 = 0.25 or 25%. Step 2: Net Profit = Gross Profit - Operating Expenses = $150,000 - $70,000 = $80,000. Net Profit Margin = Net Profit / Total Sales = $80,000 / $600,000 = 0.1333 or 13.33%.
